messages.content: How many experts does the Llama 4 Maverick model have?

{"query": "Llama 4 Maverick model experts MoE architecture", "top_k": [{"url": "https://www.emergentmind.com/topics/llama-4-maverick", "title": "Llama 4 Maverick: Advanced Multimodal MoE - Emergent Mind", "content": "* Llama 4 Maverick is a large-scale, mixture-of-experts multimodal language model that leverages 128 experts per token and a 17B active parameter framework. Llama 4 Maverick is a large-scale, mixture-of-experts (MoE) multimodal LLM released by Meta in April 2025. * **LLaMA 4 Scout and Maverick** (Apr 2025): 17B active MoE, 16\u2013128 experts, 10M context, multimodal. Maverick delivers strong empirical results, outperforming larger dense LLaMA baselines and closing a substantial fraction of the gap to closed-source SOTA models (GPT-4, PaLM) across a suite of tasks (Abdullah et al., 14 Oct 2025):. Llama 4 Maverick achieves high parameter efficiency by combining sparse gating over 128 experts per token, FP8 precision, and an extensive multimodal pretraining regime. The Maverick model exemplifies a new frontier in mixture-of-experts architectures and efficient, practical large-scale model deployment (Abdullah et al., 14 Oct 2025, Tang et al., 11 Aug 2025). 1. How does the MoE architecture in Llama 4 Maverick enhance performance compared to traditional dense models?", "score": 0.99998796, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://medium.com/@karanbhutani477/llama-4-technical-analysis-decoding-the-architecture-behind-metas-multimodal-moe-revolution-535b2775d07d", "title": "Llama 4 Technical Analysis: Decoding the Architecture Behind ...", "content": "This technical analysis breaks down Llama 4\u2019s four core innovations \u2014 the MoE architecture, early fusion multimodality, iRoPE context scaling, and FP8 training efficiency \u2014 and compares each directly to the dense transformer approach of Llama 3. **Enter Llama 4**, which tackles these challenges head-on with new approaches: a Mixture-of-Experts architecture to explode parameter count without proportional compute cost, an early-fusion multimodal design to natively handle text+images+video, an *iRoPE* positional encoding to support extremely long contexts, and **FP8** precision for efficient training and deployment. **Llama 4\u2019s solution is a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture** \u2014 effectively having a collection of specialized mini-models (\u201cexperts\u201d) and activating only a few relevant ones per token. Compared to Llama 3\u2019s dense transformer baseline, Llama 4 introduces specialist experts (MoE) to vastly expand capacity without a proportional compute hit, blends multiple input modalities from the very start for deeply integrated understanding, extends positional embeddings to support unprecedented context lengths, and leverages low-bit precision to make training and inference faster and more economical.", "score": 0.9999863, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.webai.com/blog/llama-4-webai-moe-quantization-and-the-rise-of-private-ai", "title": "Llama 4 + webAI: MoE, Quantization, and the Rise of Private AI", "content": "* **MoE architecture unlocks local performance:** Activating only ~4.25% of parameters per token, Mixture-of-Experts models like Llama 4 dramatically reduce computational needs, enabling powerful AI on accessible hardware like Apple Silicon. With models like DeepSeek and now Llama 4, the message is clear: **open, local, and private AI isn\u2019t just possible \u2014 it\u2019s smarter business.**. Let\u2019s unpack why the Llama 4 moment is way bigger than a model drop \u2014 and why it just made local-first AI the default for enterprises. With Llama 4, DeepSeek, and other open MoE models, we can now deploy state-of-the-art AI across **Apple Silicon clusters, edge environments, and private enterprise hardware** \u2014 fully outside the cloud. Our stack is optimized to take models like Llama 4 and make them run *fast, lean, and securely* on Apple hardware you already have \u2014 whether that\u2019s a fleet of Mac Studios, Minis, Pros, etc. Llama 4\u2019s release \u2014 and its open, MoE-based architecture \u2014 makes one thing clear: **state-of-the-art AI no longer requires cloud infrastructure.**.", "score": 0.9999517, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://medium.com/@tahirbalarabe2/metas-new-llama-4-s-moe-architecture-makes-ai-faster-cheaper-635339e51e10", "title": "Meta's New Llama 4's MoE Architecture Makes AI Faster & Cheaper", "content": "Llama 4 models are designed with native multimodality, employing early fusion to integrate text and vision tokens into a unified model backbone.", "score": 0.99984646, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.ibm.com/new/announcements/meta-llama-4-maverick-and-llama-4-scout-now-available-in-watsonx-ai", "title": "Meta Llama 4 Maverick and Llama 4 Scout now available in watsonx ...", "content": "# Meta Llama 4 Maverick and Llama 4 Scout now available in watsonx.ai. **IBM is excited to announce the addition of Meta\u2019s latest generation of open models, Llama 4, to** **watsonx.ai****.** Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick, the first mixture of experts (MoE) models released by Meta, provide frontier multimodal performance, high speeds, low cost, and industry leading context length. With the introduction of these latest offerings from Meta, IBM now supports a total of 13 Meta models in the expansive library of \u00a0foundation models available in watsonx.ai. Trained on 40 trillion tokens of data, Llama 4 Scout offers performance rivalling or exceeding that of models with significantly larger active parameter counts while keeping costs and latency low. ## Llama 4 models on IBM watsonx. Developers and businesses can select their preferred Llama 4 model from the extensive catalog of foundation models on IBM watsonx.ai, then fine-tune, distill and deploy it across cloud, on-premises, or edge environments of their choice. Start using Llama 4 models on watsonx.ai today. Start using Llama 4 models on watsonx.ai today.", "score": 0.9997998, "raw_content": null}]}
